name: PostHog MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official PostHog MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with PostHog's product analytics platform — querying events, trends, funnels, cohorts, feature flags, and experiments from an open-source analytics stack.
use_cases: Querying product analytics events and user behaviors from agents
Checking feature flag status and rollout percentages via agents
Analyzing funnel conversion data for product decision agents
Reading A/B experiment results from agents for automated analysis
User cohort queries for personalization and targeting agents
not_for: Infrastructure monitoring (PostHog is product analytics, not infra metrics)
Teams using Amplitude, Mixpanel without PostHog
Real-time event processing at high scale
best_when: An agent needs product analytics insights, feature flag state, or experiment results from a PostHog-instrumented product — especially in open-source/self-hosted scenarios.
avoid_when: You're using Amplitude or Mixpanel without PostHog — use their native integrations.

auth.notes: Personal API keys via POSTHOG_API_KEY. Project API key vs personal API key — personal key needed for management operations. No fine-grained scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 1M events/month, unlimited feature flags, 15K session replays
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-as-you-go after free tier; generous volume discounts
pricing.notes: PostHog OSS is free and self-hostable. Cloud is usage-based with very generous free tier. Open source with enterprise support available.

performance.rate_limits: 240 req/min per personal API key; documented

known_agent_gotchas: Project ID vs organization required for most queries — discover these first
HogQL (PostHog's SQL dialect) required for advanced analytics queries
Personal API key vs project API key — use personal for management API calls
Self-hosted PostHog has different base URLs — configure endpoint correctly
Rate limit (240/min) easily hit by analytics agents running many queries
Feature flag evaluation at API level — does not support local evaluation for agents
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Personal API keys lack scopes — full project access. Self-hosted adds data privacy benefits. SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA. Open source transparency is a security plus.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. No scope granularity on API keys. Self-hosted option provides strong data privacy. SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA. Open source codebase improves trust.
